hypotheses.description: Health outcomes indicate severe and/or worsening morbidity and/or malnutrition affecting large segments of the population.
Health outcomes indicate pockets of severe morbidity and/or malnutrition, but outcomes are not widespread. (Serious/alert)
Health outcomes are largely contained/stable, with severe outcomes limited or mitigated
Most people experience catastrophic food consumption gaps without mitigation capacities (system failure)
Some people (pockets of the population) experience catastrophic food consumption gaps without mitigation capacities
Most people experience severe food consumption gaps with or without mitigation capacities
Some people experience severe food consumption gaps with or without mitigation capacities
Food consumption gaps might exist but are not severe for most people of the population, coping strategies or assistance are largely effective in preventing acute food insecurity
Water services are severely compromised, affecting most of the population (system failure)
Water services are severely compromised, affecting pockets of the population
Water system is functioning with only minor or localised issues; most people have sufficient safe water for basic needs
Most people live in severely overcrowded, unsanitary, or unsafe conditions (system failure)
Severely overcrowded, unsanitary, or unsheltered conditions exist for some people, but are not widespread
Conditions are substandard (e.g., poor quality shelter, limited sanitation), but basic protection and hygiene are maintained for most people through coping or assistance
Conditions are meeting minimum standards for most of the population
Health / Nutrition services are severely compromised, affecting most of the population (system failure)
Health / Nutrition services are severely compromised, affecting pockets of the population
Health / Nutrition services are maintaining minimum service levels for most of the population, or severe gaps are mitigated
